# COMPONENT LIBRARY (from Superside screenshots)

# COMPONENT LIBRARY (from Superside screenshots)

Before the copy — here's the visual vocabulary. Every section references a component type.

| Component ID | Name | Description | Reference |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **C1** | Bento Grid | Mixed-size cards (1 large \+ 2 small, or 2 medium \+ 1 small, etc). Each card: colored bg \+ title \+ 1 line \+ cover image. Superside: motion ads, campaign support, AI-powered motion, etc. |  |
| **C2** | Icon Grid | 3×2 or 3×3 grid. Each cell: icon \+ bold title \+ 1-sentence description. Clean, no images. Superside: "Paid and programmatic", "Landing pages", "Meta and TikTok". |  |
| **C3** | Numbered Timeline | Vertical connected steps (1→2→3→4→5). Each: number circle \+ bold title \+ 1-sentence body. Left-aligned headline. Superside: "How we work". |  |
| **C4** | Stats Strip | 3 big numbers side by side. Number \+ label underneath. Superside: "3,000+ / 5x / 4.9/5". |  |
| **C5** | Full-Width CTA Banner | Image on right, text on left. Eyebrow \+ headline (italic emphasis) \+ body \+ button. Dark or light bg. |  |
| **C6** | Team Bento | Photo card \+ specialty card \+ quote card \+ location card \+ brands card. All in a grid. Superside: Michi Gonzalez showcase. |  |
| **C7** | Split Section | Left: eyebrow \+ large headline (italic emphasis) \+ body \+ CTA. Right: image or animation. Superside: "Full-stack expertise". |  |
| **C8** | Dark Process | Dark background. Left: headline \+ body \+ CTA \+ stats. Right: numbered timeline with eyebrow tags per step. Superside: "How we work with you". |  |
| **C9** | Testimonial \+ Stat Bento | Mixed grid: stat card (big %) \+ testimonial card (quote \+ photo \+ name \+ company logo) \+ stat card. Asymmetric layout. Superside: "Creative Excellence". |  |
| **C10** | Global/Team Hero | Full-bleed image bg \+ headline \+ body \+ glass-effect overlay pills with facts. Superside: "Our Global Team". |  |
| **C11** | Hero with 3-card strip | Headline \+ body \+ CTA, then 3 cards below with italic emphasis \+ cover image. Superside: homepage. |  |

### SECTION 5 — Stats Strip \[C4\]

**$44** — return on every $1 spent on restaurant email. The highest-ROI channel in hospitality. Nothing else comes close.

**70%** — of first-time diners never return. The problem isn't your food. It's the silence after they leave.

**2.7x** — more guests retained by brands using direct 1:1 engagement vs those relying on broad marketing. The gap is widening.

# SERVICE PAGES

# SERVICE PAGE TEMPLATE

Every service page uses the same component flow:

1\. Hero \[C7: Split\] — eyebrow \+ headline \+ body \+ CTA | 3-format cover (H/V/S)

2\. What's included \[C2: Icon Grid\] — 6 cells, icon \+ title \+ 1 line

3\. Value Props \[C1: Bento Grid\] — 3-6 mixed-size cards with covers

4\. Insight Cards \[C9: Testimonial+Stat Bento\] — 2-4 cards, reusable

5\. How It Works \[C3: Numbered Timeline\] — 3-5 steps

6\. Stats Strip \[C4\] — 2-3 big numbers

7\. Related Case Study — card link

8\. Other Services — 3 sibling cards

9\. CTA \[C5: Full-Width Banner\]

---

# Brand Strategy & Creative Direction

## Brand Strategy & Creative Direction — /services/brand-strategy

### Hero \[C7\]

| Field | Copy |
| :---- | :---- |
| Eyebrow | GENERATE DEMAND |
| Headline | Before anyone sees your content, *they need to feel your brand* |
| Body | We define how you look, sound, and position — so everything else works harder. |
| Cover H | `[COVER: mood board flat lay — colors, type samples, reference images]` |
| Cover V | `[COVER: brand guidelines document on iPad]` |
| Cover S | `[COVER: logo + color system close-up]` |

### What's included \[Icon Grid\]-PERKS

| COVER | Title | Description-Dont do nothing with it now  |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
|  | Visual identity | Colors, typography, photo style, brand marks — your complete visual system. |
|  | Voice & tone | How your brand sounds across captions, emails, menus, ads, and signage. |
|  | Creative direction | A playbook your photographer, editor, and ads team follow without asking. |
|  | Competitive positioning | What separates you from every other restaurant in your market. Defined, not assumed. |
|  |  |  |
|  | Multi-location scaling | Same brand, adapted per location. Consistency without sameness. |

**BUILDING BRAND IDENTITY**

Your brand is the first thing people *feel before they taste anything.*

When your Instagram, your Google, your menu, and your email all feel like different restaurants — you're losing trust at every touchpoint.

We build the visual identity, voice, and creative direction that makes everything click. One look. One voice. Every platform. So your team, your photographer, and your ads all follow the same playbook without asking.

**■ COVER:** *Phone showing consistent IG grid across 3 locations*

### 2-Value Props \[C1: Bento Grid\]

| Size | Title | Copy | Cover |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| LARGE | A brand that *makes sense at first glance* | Your IG, your Google, your menu, your email — if they don't feel like the same restaurant, you're leaking trust every day. We build the system that locks it in. | `[COVER: 4-panel showing same brand across IG, Google, email, menu]` |
| MEDIUM | Your photographer isn't *guessing anymore* | We hand your team a playbook. Angles, lighting, mood, crop ratios, color grading — documented so every shoot matches the last one, even with a different crew. | `[COVER: creative brief with photo examples]` |
| SMALL | Positioning that survives *a competitor opening next door* | We don't write taglines. We define the space your brand owns in people's heads — so when a new place opens nearby, your regulars don't even flinch. | `[COVER: competitive landscape diagram — simple]` |

### 

| Type | Content | Visual |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| STAT (green) | **23%** revenue increase attributed to consistent branding across channels. Not creativity — consistency. | — |
| STAT (cream) | **5–7** impressions before someone remembers a brand. Make each one count by looking the same every time. | — |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |

### Stats \[C4\]

| Number | Label |
| :---- | :---- |
| **23%** | Revenue lift from consistent branding |
| **5–7** | Impressions to brand recall |
| **80%** | Of recognition driven by color \+ visual consistency ,formats and tone of voice.  |

### 3- How It Works \[C3: Timeline\]

| \# | Title | Body |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Brand audit | We review everything — your current visual assets, competitors, audience, and market positioning. |
| 2 | Strategy workshop | A working session where we define your visual system, voice, and competitive position together. |
| 3 | System build | We create the full identity system — logo refinement, color, type, photo direction, brand guidelines. |
| 4 | Team handoff | Your brand book gets shared with your photographer, editor, social team, and printer. Everyone aligned. |
| 5 | Ongoing stewardship | We apply and protect the brand across every channel, every month. It compounds. |

# Film & Photo Production — /services/film-photo

## Film & Photo Production — /services/film-photo-\> 

### Hero \[C7\]

| Field | Copy |
| :---- | :---- |
| Eyebrow | GENERATE DEMAND |
| Headline | The food is doing the hard part. *We just make sure people see it.* |
| Body | On-location production built around your menu, your space, your people. |
| Cover H | `[COVER: wide BTS — camera rig, chef plating, restaurant interior]` |
| Cover V | `[COVER: moody food close-up — single dish, dramatic light]` |
| Cover S | `[COVER: overhead plate shot — hands reaching in]` |

### What's included \[C2\]-\>PERKS

### 

| Cover | Title | Description-Dont do nothing with it now  |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
|  | On-location photo | Shot inside your restaurant with your actual food and team. No studio. |
|  | Reels & video | Short-form content built for social performance — hooks, pacing, music. |
|  | Strategic shot lists | Every frame planned against the content calendar before we arrive. |
|  | Visual library | A bank of assets for social, ads, website, Google, and print. |
|  | Signature style dev | When standard doesn't fit, we find what does. Then we own it. |
|  | Multi-POV coverage | Single flash, diffuser, overhead, 45°, detail, environment — every item covered. |

**CAPTURING WHAT'S REAL**

Your food already looks incredible. *Your content should too.*

Stock photos and phone shots worked five years ago. Today, 40% of people visit a restaurant after seeing food photos online. The bar moved.

We shoot on location — your kitchen, your plates, your team. Every frame planned against the content calendar. One shoot day gives you Reels, carousels, hero shots, Google photos, and ad creative. Six angles of one dish gives you six months of content.

**■ COVER:** *BTS wide shot — camera rig, chef plating, warm light*

### Stats \[C4\]

| Number | Label |
| :---- | :---- |
| **40%** | Visit after seeing food photos |
| **6** | Different angles of one dish \= 6 months of content |
| **2.3x** | More saves on food Reels vs. other categories |

### Value Props \[C1: Bento\]

| Size | Title | Copy | Cover |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| LARGE | Shoot once, *use everywhere* | One production day gives you Reels, carousels, hero shots, stories, graphics, Google photos, and ad creative. We don't shoot for Instagram — we shoot for the entire system. | `[COVER: grid showing 8+ content pieces from one shoot day]` |
| MEDIUM | The appetizer that *changed a brand's entire look* | At Aahaa, a single appetizer shoot with a diffuser and deep shadows replaced their entire visual direction. That pivot didn't come from a trend — it came from understanding the food. | `[COVER: Aahaa appetizer — before (flat flash) vs after (moody diffused)]` |
| SMALL | Different photos *you must have of every item* | Not just the hero angle. The cut, the steam, the hand reaching in, the table context, the ingredient close-up. 6 photos of one dish gives you 6 months of content. | `[COVER: 6-panel grid of same dish, different angles]` |
| SMALL | Photos of *no food* that matter more than food | Your team laughing. The bar at golden hour. A hand wiping down a table. The stuff that makes someone feel what it's like to be there — not just what's on the plate. | `[COVER: collage — team culture, environment, vibe shots]` |

### 

| Type | Content | Visual |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| STAT (green) | **40%** of people visit a restaurant after seeing food photos online. Not ads. Not reviews. Photos. | — |
| STAT (cream) | **2.3x** more saves on Reels with food content vs. any other Instagram category. Saves \= intent. | — |

# Social Content

## Social Content — /services/social-content

### Hero \[C7\]

| Eyebrow | GENERATE DEMAND | | Headline | Posting isn't a strategy. *Never was.* | | Body | Platform-native content built around business goals, not trending audio. | | Cover H/V/S | `[COVER: content calendar view]` `[COVER: phone showing client feed]` `[COVER: engagement analytics]` |

### What's included \[C2\]-\>PERKS

| Title | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Content archetypes | Crave, vibe, chef POV, social proof, education, culture — strategic variety. |
| Platform-native strategy | IG, TikTok, Facebook, YouTube — each treated as its own channel. |
| Content calendar | Built around your business goals and seasonal pushes. |
| Compilations vs. process | Mood collections and start-to-finish narratives. Two styles, two editing approaches. |
| Performance tracking | Tied to real outcomes — not follower counts. |
| Series development | When a theme+pillar combo works, it graduates into a named recurring series. |

### 

**STRATEGIC CONTENT CREATION**

Posting without a plan *is just noise with a filter.*

74% of diners use social media to decide where to eat. Your feed isn't a gallery — it's a decision engine.

We build content around archetypes — crave, vibe, chef POV, social proof, education, culture. Each platform gets its own approach. Everything ties back to what drives visits, not vanity metrics.

**■ COVER:** *Phone showing archetype-driven IG feed with labels*

### Value Props \[C1\]

| Size | Title | Copy | Cover |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| LARGE | Your content *has a system behind it* | Every post belongs to an archetype. Every archetype has a job. Crave makes them hungry. Vibe makes them feel it. Chef POV builds authority. Social proof builds trust. Nothing random. | `[COVER: archetype grid — 6 post types labeled and color-coded]` |
| MEDIUM | The brunch campaign that *moved actual revenue* | T'ÄHÄ. One focused push. One theme. One month. The result wasn't more followers — it was more people sitting down on Sunday mornings. That's what a campaign does vs. random content. | `[COVER: T'ÄHÄ brunch content carousel + revenue metric overlay]` |
| SMALL | Compilations vs. process — *know the difference* | Compilations are mood-driven collections (no sequence required). Process formats are start-to-finish narratives (order matters). Using the wrong one makes great content feel off. | `[COVER: side-by-side example — compilation cut vs. process cut]` |

### 

| Type | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| STAT | **74%** of diners use social media to decide where to eat. Your content is their first impression and their last check before they walk in. |
| STAT | Carousel posts generate **3x** more engagement than single images for restaurants. Swipe \= curiosity \= intent. |
| CASE | **T'ÄHÄ brunch** — one campaign. One theme. Measurable revenue lift. → /work/taha |

# Social Media Management

## Social Media Management — /services/social-management

### Hero \[C7\]

| Eyebrow | GENERATE DEMAND | | Headline | Your brand should be *present, not just posting.* | | Body | Daily engagement, community management, and the small interactions that build real followings. | | Cover H/V/S | `[COVER: DM thread]` `[COVER: comment section with replies]` `[COVER: story interaction]` |

### What's included \[C2\]-\>PERKS

| Title | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Community management | Comments, DMs, story replies — daily. Your brand talks back. |
| Calendar execution | Scheduling, publishing, cross-posting — handled completely. |
| Engagement strategy | Who to follow, what to comment on, which hashtags matter in your market. |
| Social listening | Mentions, tags, stories — we catch every time someone talks about you. |
| Reporting | Weekly activity, monthly performance, quarterly trends. |
| Crisis response | Negative comment? Viral complaint? We have a protocol before you even see it. |

**ALWAYS-ON BRAND PRESENCE**

The comment you didn't reply to *cost you a table tonight.*

Brands that respond within one hour see 7x more conversions. Most restaurants never respond at all.

We handle daily engagement across every platform — comments, DMs, story replies, mentions, tags. Your brand stays present even when you're slammed on a Friday night.

**■ COVER:** *Comment thread showing thoughtful brand reply*

### Value Props \[C1\]

| Size | Title | Copy | Cover |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| LARGE | The comment you didn't respond to *cost you a table tonight* | 7x more conversions from brands that respond within 1 hour. Most restaurants never respond at all. We respond to every single one — with your brand voice, not a template. | `[COVER: comment thread showing thoughtful brand reply]` |
| MEDIUM | Social listening that *catches what you miss* | Someone posted a story from your restaurant and tagged you. Someone complained about wait time in a comment. Someone asked if you're open on Mondays. We catch all of it. | `[COVER: social listening feed with tagged content]` |
| MEDIUM | Your feed stays active *even when you're slammed* | You run the restaurant. We run the brand. Content goes out on schedule, DMs get answered, comments get replied to — whether you had a good Tuesday or a disaster. | `[COVER: calendar with auto-published content]` |

### Stats \[C4\]

| Number | Label |
| :---- | :---- |

| **40%** | Follower retention increase with active community management |
| **100%** | Response rate we maintain for every client, every platform |

# Paid Media Strategies — /services/paid-media

## Paid Media Strategies — /services/paid-media

### Hero \[C7\]

| Eyebrow | GENERATE DEMAND | | Headline | We don't boost posts. *We build campaigns.* | | Body | Budget behind creative that already proved it resonates. Every dollar tracked. | | Cover H/V/S | `[COVER: ad manager dashboard]` `[COVER: ad creative on phone]` `[COVER: campaign results card]` |

### What's included \[C2\]-\>PERKS

| Title | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Proven creative first | We only run ads on content with strong organic signal. |
| Geo-targeting | People within driving distance, during dining decision hours. |
| A/B testing | Multiple creative and copy variations. Optimize toward conversions. |
| Cross-platform | Meta, TikTok, Google — wherever your audience makes decisions. |
| Transparent reporting | Reach, clicks, CTR, CPM, cost per action — plain language. |
| Paid vs organic view | See the relationship between what's earned and what's amplified. |

**AMPLIFYING WHAT WORKS**

We never put money *behind a guess.*

Most agencies boost random posts and call it a campaign. We only run ads on content that already proved it resonates. Data before dollars.

Every campaign is geo-targeted to people within driving distance during dining decisions. Restaurant CPM averages $6–$12 — half of most industries. Your ad dollar goes further here.

**■ COVER:** *Organic post analytics → same post running as ad with results*

### Value Props \[C1\]

| Size | Title | Copy | Cover |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| LARGE | We never put money behind a guess | Every ad we run started as organic content that already proved it resonates. We look at saves, shares, comments — then we amplify. Most agencies start with the budget. We start with the proof. | `[COVER: organic post analytics → arrow → same post as ad with results]` |
| MEDIUM | Geo-targeted to the people *who are deciding right now* | Your ad reaches someone 4 miles away, at 5:30pm, scrolling Instagram while thinking "what's for dinner tonight." That's the window. We build for it. | `[COVER: map with radius highlighting + time-of-day overlay]` |
| SMALL | Restaurant CPM is *half* of most industries | Your paid dollar goes further in hospitality than almost any other category. $6–$12 CPM vs. $15–$25 for SaaS. We make sure you're not overpaying for reach you could earn cheaper. | `[COVER: CPM comparison bar chart — restaurants vs other industries]` |

### Stats \[C4\]

| Number | Label |
| :---- | :---- |
| **3x** | Higher conversion on geo-targeted restaurant ads |
| **$6–12** | Restaurant CPM — half the industry average |
| **100%** | Of our ad creative starts with organic proof |

# Google & Local SEO — /services/google-seo

## Google & Local SEO — /services/google-seo

### Hero \[C7\]

| Eyebrow | CONVERT DEMAND | | Headline | If they can't find you, *they can't choose you.* | | Body | Your Google profile is the new front door. We make sure it converts. | | Cover H/V/S | `[COVER: Google Maps pin]` `[COVER: GBP on phone]` `[COVER: search results with client]` |

### What's included \[C2\]-\>PERKS

| Title | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| GBP optimization | Every field, every location, always current. |
| Local search strategy | Category \+ keyword targeting for local pack visibility. |
| Weekly GBP posts | Updates that keep your profile active and ranking. |
| Search tracking | Discovery vs. direct, views, direction requests, calls — measured monthly. |
| Q\&A management | Questions answered before prospects have to ask twice. |
| Multi-location consistency | NAP, hours, categories — matched across every listing. |

### 

**OWNING LOCAL SEARCH**

46% of Google searches *are someone nearby deciding right now.*

Your Google profile is being judged right now. Someone searched “tacos near me” and three profiles showed up. Yours has four photos from 2022\.

We optimize every field across every location. We publish weekly updates. We track discovery vs. direct searches, direction requests, and phone calls. You'll know if your brand is growing or coasting.

**■ COVER:** *Side-by-side GBP comparison — neglected vs. optimized*

### Value Props \[C1\]

| Size | Title | Copy | Cover |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| LARGE | Your Google profile is *being judged right now* | Someone just searched "tacos near me." Three profiles showed up. Yours has 4 photos from 2022, last post 6 months ago, 3 unanswered questions. The competitor's profile is full, active, and fresh. Guess who gets the visit. | `[COVER: side-by-side GBP comparison — neglected vs. optimized]` |
| MEDIUM | The restaurant that posts weekly on Google *gets 2x the actions* | Not social. Google. Posts, offers, updates — directly on your business profile. Most restaurants don't know this exists. Their competitors do. | `[COVER: GBP post examples + profile actions chart]` |
| SMALL | We track the searches *that actually matter* | "Best tacos KC" vs. "Taco Naco KC" — discovery vs. direct. The ratio tells us whether your brand is growing or coasting. We track it monthly. | `[COVER: discovery vs direct search trend chart]` |

# Reputation Management & Reviews

## Reputation Management & Reviews — /services/reputation

### Hero \[C7\]

| Eyebrow | CONVERT DEMAND | | Headline | Your rating is your resume. *We manage it like one.* | | Body | Every review answered. Every rating tracked. Every location covered. | | Cover H/V/S | `[COVER: review dashboard]` `[COVER: 5-star review on phone]` `[COVER: rating trend chart]` |

### 

### **What's Included( Just Use the title )**\-\>PERKS 

* ### **Review response** — every Google review answered within 24 hours, in your brand voice

* ### **Rating monitoring** — trends, volume, and star distribution tracked across all locations

* ### **Sentiment analysis** — what people praise and what they complain about, turned into patterns your kitchen can act on

* ### **Survey pipeline** — post-visit survey via SMS. Score 4-5? Guided to Google review. Score 1-3? Caught privately before it goes public.

* ### **Competitive monitoring** — how your ratings compare to competitors in your zip code

* ### **Response rate tracking** — your response percentage, average response time, and unresponded count flagged

### 

### 

### **PROTECTING YOUR RATING**

A single star on Google *is worth 5–9% more revenue.*

88% of diners trust online reviews as much as a personal recommendation. Your rating isn't just a number — it's a P\&L line item.

We respond to every Google review within 24 hours in your brand voice. We track sentiment patterns across all locations. Our survey pipeline catches unhappy guests privately before they post a 1-star.

**■ COVER:** *Review feed with response rate stats overlaid*

### Value Props \[C1\]

| Size | Title | Copy | Cover |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| LARGE | The review you didn't respond to *is still sitting there* | 53% of customers expect a response within 7 days. Only 36% of restaurants respond at all. Every unanswered review tells the next prospect: "this place doesn't care." We answer every single one — within 24 hours, in your brand voice. | `[COVER: review feed with response rate stats overlay]` |
| MEDIUM | One star is worth *5–9% more revenue* | Not a marketing stat. A P\&L stat. A 4.2 to a 4.5 on Google can mean the difference between a slow Tuesday and a full house. We build the systems that move the number. | `[COVER: star rating → revenue impact chart]` |
| MEDIUM | We catch the unhappy guest *before they go public* | Our survey-to-review pipeline sends a 1-question check after every order. Score 4-5? Guided to Google. Score 1-3? Caught privately. Problem resolved before it becomes a 1-star review. | `[COVER: survey flow — order → SMS → score → branch]` |
| SMALL | Sentiment patterns that *tell your kitchen what to fix* | When 14 reviews mention "long wait" in the same month, that's not a marketing problem — it's an operations insight. We surface the patterns your team needs to see. | `[COVER: word frequency chart from review sentiment]` |

### Stats \[C4\]

| Number | Label |
| :---- | :---- |
| **88%** | Trust reviews like personal recommendations |
| **5–9%** | Revenue increase per 1-star improvement |
| **24hr** | Our maximum response time on every review |

92% of consumers trust earned media over traditional advertising. One authentic post from the right creator outperforms a month of ads.

# Email & SMS Marketing — /services/email-sms

## Email & SMS Marketing — /services/email-sms

### Hero \[C7\]

| Eyebrow | RETAIN DEMAND | | Headline | The guest who already trusts you *is your most valuable audience.* | | Body | Direct communication. Not rented reach. $42 return per $1 spent. | | Cover H/V/S | `[COVER: email on desktop]` `[COVER: SMS on phone]` `[COVER: email template preview]` |

### **Email & SMS Marketing — What's Included (( Just Use the title )**\-\>PERKS

### 

* **Automated flows** — welcome, lapsed reactivation, birthday, post-visit follow-up, VIP recognition — all triggered by real behavior  
* **Segmented campaigns** — right message, right segment, right time. New vs returning vs lapsed vs VIP  
* **Revenue attribution** — every send tracked against POS orders within 14 days. You see exactly which email drove which orders  
* **SMS \+ email together** — two channels, different jobs, same strategy. Not competing — complementing  
* **List health** — subscriber growth, opt-outs, bounce rates, deliverability monitored. Your audience stays clean  
* **Campaign reporting** — open rates, click rates, revenue per send, best day and time — all in your dashboard

**DIRECT GUEST RELATIONSHIPS**

Segmented emails drive *760% more revenue than blasts.*

Not 7%. Not 76%. Seven hundred and sixty percent. Because “20% off everything” sent to everyone is noise.

“We miss you — here's your favorite order, 20% off” sent to a lapsed guest at 5pm Thursday — that's a sale. We build automated flows, track every send against your POS, and show you exactly which email drove which orders. $42 return per $1.

**■ COVER:** *Automated flow diagram with revenue tags per step*

### Value Props \[C1\]

| Size | Title | Copy | Cover |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| LARGE | Segmented sends drive *760% more revenue* than blasts | Not 7%. Not 76%. Seven hundred and sixty percent. Because "20% off everything" sent to everyone is noise. "We miss you — here's your favorite order, 20% off" sent to a lapsed guest at 5pm on a Thursday? That's a sale. | `[COVER: blast vs. segmented send — open rate + revenue comparison]` |
| MEDIUM | Your welcome flow *earns money while you sleep* | New subscriber → welcome email (day 0\) → location guide (day 2\) → first-visit offer (day 5\) → menu highlight (day 10). Automated. Triggered. Revenue attributed. You never touch it. | `[COVER: automated flow diagram with revenue tags per step]` |
| MEDIUM | Every send *tracked to actual orders* | We match Twilio send timestamps to Toast POS orders within a 14-day window. You see exactly how many orders came from that Thursday email. Not estimated. Measured. | `[COVER: attribution table — send → orders → revenue]` |
| SMALL | The birthday email that *actually works* | Not "Happy birthday\! Here's 10% off." We send it 3 days early with a specific menu recommendation based on their past orders. Redemption rate: significantly above industry average. | `[COVER: birthday email mockup]` |

### Stats \[C4\]

| Number | Label |
| :---- | :---- |
| **$42** | Return per $1 on restaurant email |
| **760%** | More revenue from segmented vs. blast emails |
| **14 days** | Attribution window — send to POS order |

Relationships, not transactions. | | Cover H/V/S | `[COVER: CRM dashboard]` `[COVER: guest profile card]` `[COVER: segment donut chart]` |

### **CRM & Loyalty — What's Included (( Just Use the title )**\-\>PERKS

### 

* **Guest segmentation** — new, returning, VIP, at-risk, lapsed, big spenders — every guest tagged and treated differently  
* **Lifecycle automations** — triggered by real behavior: 5th visit, 60-day absence, birthday, first order, big spend  
* **Survey → review pipeline** — happy guests guided to Google review, unhappy guests caught privately before it goes public  
* **VIP recognition** — your best customers get acknowledged automatically, not ignored  
* **Lapsed reactivation** — 60 days no visit triggers an SMS, 22% come back, $47 average return spend  
* **Direct ownership** — you own the guest list, not Meta, not DoorDash, not any third-party platform

### Value Props \[C1\]

| Size | Title | Copy | Cover |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| LARGE | 78% of your guests *never come back* — not because the food was bad | Because nobody asked them to. No follow-up. No "thanks for coming." No reason to choose you over the new place that opened last week. We build the systems that make the second visit feel inevitable. | `[COVER: churn funnel — visit 1 → silence → lost → vs → visit 1 → follow-up → return → regular]` |
| MEDIUM | Your VIP *doesn't know they're a VIP* | 5+ visits. Top 10% spend. Orders every other week. And they've never received a single acknowledgment from your brand. We fix that — automatically. | `[COVER: VIP recognition email/SMS mockup]` |
| MEDIUM | The lapsed guest *reactivation that pays for itself* | 60 days since last visit → triggered SMS: "It's been a minute. Your usual?" → 22% reactivation rate → average reactivated guest spends $47 in first return visit. The flow costs nothing to run after setup. | `[COVER: reactivation flow with conversion metrics]` |
| SMALL | You own the relationship. *Not Meta. Not DoorDash. You.* | Your email list doesn't disappear when an algorithm changes. Your SMS subscribers don't vanish when a platform goes down. Direct customer relationships are the only asset that compounds without a platform tax. | `[COVER: owned vs rented audience comparison — simple diagram]` |

| **5–7x** | More expensive to acquire a new customer than retain |
| **78%** | Of guests never return without a system |

# Analytics & Reporting — /services/analytics

## Analytics & Reporting — /services/analytics

### Hero \[C7\]

| Eyebrow | RETAIN DEMAND | | Headline | You can't fix *what you can't see.* | | Body | One dashboard. Sales, social, email, reviews, campaigns. Every dollar accounted for. | | Cover H/V/S | `[COVER: full dashboard]` `[COVER: mobile dashboard]` `[COVER: monthly report page]` |

### **Analytics & Reporting — What's Included ( Just Use the title )**\-\>PERKS

### 

* **Direct \+ Influence ROI** — email sent Tuesday, 14 orders by Friday, $658 attributed (direct). 340K impressions × 0.5% × $18 check \= $30,600 modeled (influence). Both numbers, every month.  
* **POS integration** — real revenue matched to campaigns within 14 days, not guesses  
* **Platform aggregation** — social, Google, email, SMS, surveys, all in one dashboard  
* **Monthly branded reports** — visual, presentation-ready, hand to your investor and say "this is what we delivered"  
* **Performance tracking** — what drives visits and orders, not follower counts  
* **KPI monitoring** — impressions, engagement, conversions, revenue per channel, all visible

**SEEING THE FULL PICTURE**

Two kinds of ROI — *because one isn't enough.*

Direct ROI: email sent Tuesday, 14 orders by Friday, $658 attributed. Influence ROI: 340K impressions × 0.5% × $18 avg check \= $30,600 modeled. You get both numbers.

We built a dashboard that pulls from your POS, social, email, Google, and surveys — all in one interface. Monthly reports are branded and presentation-ready. Hand it to your investor and say “this is what our marketing team delivered.”

# TACO NACO

### **TACO NACO KC**

---

**Cover:** `[best hero shot- On Drive (Provisional)  ]`

#### **HERO**

**Client:** Taco Naco KC 

**Result headline:** 3 locations. 23K followers. Over 1.2 million impressions. One brand. 

**Service tags:** Brand Strategy · Content · Social · Google · Reputation · Analytics 

#### **CREATIVE — PHOTOS**

`[Carousel — swipe through->On drive)` 

---

#### **THE STORY**

Taco Naco KC has three locations — Overland Park, Westport, and State Line. Great food, loyal crowd, but online the brand was all over the place. No consistent look. No real strategy. No way to know what was working.

We came in December 2025 and built the whole thing from scratch. One brand direction. One content system. One team managing every platform, every location, every day. We started shooting monthly, launched platforms they weren't on, and connected their Google profiles to a real strategy.

Five months in — 23K followers across every platform, over 713K impressions on social, and 504K impressions on Google alone across all three locations. Over 1,000 pieces of content published. The brand finally looks and sounds like one brand, no matter where you find it.

---

#### **CREATIVE — VIDEOS**

`[Carousel — swipe through-On drive]`

---

#### **RESULTS**

| Number | Label |
| ----- | ----- |
| **23K** | followers across all platforms (started at \~5.9K) |
| **1.2M+** | total impressions (social \+ Google across 3 locations) |
| **1,031** | pieces of content published in 5 months |
| **504K** | Google impressions (168K per location × 3\) |

---

#### **QUOTE**

`[PLACEHOLDER — Fernanda Chef , Owner, Taco Naco KC]` `[Headshot needed]`

---

#### **NEXT → CTA**

# T’ÄHÄ Mexican Kitchen

**ATRIUM AGENCY  ·  CASE STUDY**

**T’ÄHÄ Mexican Kitchen**

*Kansas City, MO  ·  Fine Dining  ·  Full-Service Engagement*

| *No paid ads. No celebrity backing. No existing audience. Just strategy, content, and a multi-step funnel — and we sold out Michelin-star dinners, built a 30% email open rate, and turned a restaurant into a brand that creators choose to be associated with.* |
| :---- |

**RESULTS AT A GLANCE**

| 5.24M+ TOTAL IMPRESSIONS *\+544% period over period* | 30% EMAIL OPEN RATE *2× industry average* | 217K+ CROSS-PLATFORM ON ONE OFFER *$1 Oysters — IG \+ TikTok combined* | \~100K EARNED UGC IMPRESSIONS *Zero production cost* |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |

| 4+ SOLD-OUT EVENTS *Organic only — zero paid ads* | 537K GOOGLE BUSINESS REACH *\+508% year over year* | 11.8K INSTAGRAM FOLLOWERS *\+1,392 net gained* | 43.2K GBP ACTION CLICKS *\+193% — calls, maps, website* |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |

**HOW WE DID IT**

| 01 | We built a multi-step funnel — not just posts Every campaign ran a full cycle: awareness content to build anticipation, PR outreach to earned media, email sequences to convert interest into reservations, and CTAs embedded at every touchpoint. The result was consistent, predictable reservation flow — not random spikes. |
| :---: | :---- |

| 02 | PR \+ email sold out Michelin-star events with no paid spend For the Mar & Tierra collaboration with Chef Alberto Ferruz (2 Michelin Stars, BonAmb, Spain) — the first event of its caliber in Kansas City — we ran targeted PR outreach, a carousel campaign, Story countdowns, and a multi-step email sequence. Both nights sold out. Same result for the T’ÄHÄ Takeover series. Every major event reached capacity through earned channels alone. |
| :---: | :---- |

| 30% | Email open rate — 2x the hospitality industry average Our email marketing maintained a 30% open rate throughout the engagement — roughly double the hospitality industry benchmark of 15–18%. Built on a multi-step funnel strategy with clear CTAs, segmented sends, and event-driven sequences that generated consistent, trackable reservation volume. |
| :---: | :---- |

| 217K+ | One offer. Every platform. Compounding reach. The $1 Oysters Wednesday campaign shows what cross-platform execution looks like in practice. A single recurring offer generated 33K+ impressions on Instagram, 33K+ on TikTok, additional reach on Facebook and Stories — all compounding week over week. One idea, multiplied across channels, became the Plaza’s most-anticipated weekly standing. |
| :---: | :---- |

| \~100K | We built a brand creators want to belong to T’ÄHÄ became a destination for content creators independently of the restaurant’s own channels. Creators visited specifically to shoot content there — drawn by the aesthetic, the culinary prestige, and the cultural cachet of the brand. That organic activity generated an estimated 100K+ additional impressions at zero production cost. When a brand reaches this stage, marketing compounds on itself. |
| :---: | :---- |

| 01 | We created the tagline — and it stuck “Crafting Mexican Excellence” was originated by Atrium. It now appears across all channels, printed menus, event materials, and in-house signage. A brand voice document, tone guidelines, and consistent copy standards ensure every piece of content — from a Tuesday reel to a Michelin-star press release — sounds like the same restaurant. |
| :---: | :---- |

# DCOP

# **DON CHUY’S FRESH MÉX & CANTINA**

### **From New Market Entry to Multi-Location Growth**

## **\+839% IMPRESSIONS. \+302% INSTAGRAM GROWTH. \+595% CUSTOMER ACTIONS.**

While competitors focused on food photography alone, we positioned Don Chuy’s around what customers were actually buying: the experience.

By showcasing the atmosphere, bar program, social energy, signature drinks, weekly promotions, and the overall destination appeal of the restaurant, we helped transform awareness into measurable business growth—supporting a period that culminated in the brand opening its fourth location.  

---

## **RESULTS AT A GLANCE(elije los que se vean mas relevantes con porcentaje o numero)**

* **Instagram grew from roughly 1,305 to 5,253 followers (+302%)**    
* **876,640 total impressions generated (+839%)**    
* **401,940 Instagram impressions (+1,430%)**    
* **30,620 total interactions (+610%)**    
* **22,860 customer actions from Google Business (+595%)**    
* **10,400 website visits from Google (+4,671%)**    
* **2,481 phone calls generated (+234%)**    
* **9,975 direction requests (+329%)**    
* **Instagram content interactions up \+987%**    
* **Instagram Reel interactions up \+9,108%**

**\+839% Total Impressions**  
876,640 organic impressions generated across platforms. 

**\+302% Instagram Audience Growth**  
From approximately 1,300 followers to more than 5,200 followers. 

**\+1,430% Instagram Reach**  
Over 401,000 impressions generated organically. 

**\+610% Total Engagement**  
More than 30,000 customer interactions. 

**\+595% Google Business Actions**  
Website visits, phone calls, and direction requests surged. 

**\+4,671% Website Visits**  
Social visibility translated into customer intent. 

**\+234% Phone Calls**  
Direct customer inquiries increased significantly. 

**\+329% Direction Requests**  
Thousands of customers actively sought the location. 

---

## **HOW WE DID IT**

### **We Sold The Destination, Not The Menu**

Most restaurant marketing focuses on dishes.

We focused on the environment, atmosphere, bar experience, design, and energy of the restaurant—creating demand for the experience itself.

### **We Built Signature Weekly Traffic Drivers**

Taco Tuesday, Ladies Night, Happy Hour, Lazy Thursday, margarita campaigns, and seasonal promotions gave customers recurring reasons to visit throughout the week.

### **We Turned The Bar Program Into A Growth Engine**

Cocktails, margarita flights, specialty drinks, and visual beverage content consistently became some of the highest-performing assets across platforms.

### **We Positioned Don Chuy’s As A Category Leader**

Rather than competing as another Mexican restaurant, we established Don Chuy’s as one of the most visually recognizable and experience-driven dining destinations in the market.

# OSPZ

# **OLD SHAWNEE PIZZA**

### **Revitalizing a Kansas City Institution Through Digital Marketing**

## **1.66 MILLION IMPRESSIONS. 24,000+ CUSTOMER ACTIONS. $250,000+ ANNUAL REVENUE INFLUENCED.**

Old Shawnee Pizza wasn’t a new restaurant trying to get noticed.

It was already one of the most recognizable independent restaurant brands in Kansas City.

For more than 55 years, the Walker family built a loyal following through great food, strong community ties, and a reputation that generations of customers trusted.

The challenge wasn’t awareness.

The challenge was translating decades of local goodwill into a modern digital presence.

When DCOP partnered with Old Shawnee Pizza, the brand had a compelling story, loyal customers, a thriving bar program, and two established locations—but very little of that was being consistently communicated online.

Our objective was to turn Old Shawnee Pizza’s legacy into a growth asset.

By highlighting the restaurant’s history, showcasing founder Joe Walker’s legacy through William Walker, promoting signature menu items, featuring the bar experience, and building recurring promotional campaigns, we transformed social media into a modern extension of the brand.

The result was increased visibility, stronger customer engagement, measurable business activity, and a renewed connection between a Kansas City institution and the next generation of customers.

---

# **RESULTS AT A GLANCE**

### **1.66 Million+ Organic Impressions**

Generated across Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, and Google Business Profile.  

### **56,280+ Customer Interactions**

Creating consistent engagement across all digital channels.  

### **28,915 Combined Followers**

Across Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok.  

### **1,915 Pieces of Content Published**

Building one of the most active restaurant content ecosystems in the Kansas City market.  

### **313,080 Google Business Impressions**

Helping customers discover the restaurant when actively searching for dining options.  

### **24,180 Customer Actions**

Including website visits, phone calls, and requests for directions.  

### **84,000+ Monthly Email Opens**

Driven through automated CRM campaigns, promotions, and retention marketing.

### **$21,500+ Average Monthly Revenue Influenced**

Generated through email marketing, CRM automation, and customer retention campaigns.

### **$258,000+ Estimated Annual Revenue Influence**

From owned marketing channels alone.

---

# **HOW WE DID IT**

## **We Turned A Legacy Into A Marketing Asset**

Most restaurants talk about their food.

Old Shawnee Pizza had something more valuable: history.

We built content around the story of Joe Walker, the family legacy, and William Walker’s leadership, creating a narrative that competitors simply couldn’t replicate.

This transformed Old Shawnee Pizza from another local restaurant into a brand with authenticity, heritage, and personality.

---

## **We Positioned The Restaurant As A Community Gathering Place**

The strongest brands aren’t built around products.

They’re built around experiences.

Our content highlighted the atmosphere, neighborhood culture, bar program, live events, and social environment that customers already loved.

The result was content that felt personal, local, and relevant.

---

## **We Made William Walker The Face Of The Brand**

People connect with people.

By featuring William throughout content, promotions, kitchen stories, behind-the-scenes videos, and menu features, we gave customers a face they could connect with.

This increased trust, strengthened engagement, and reinforced the family-owned identity of the business.

---

## **We Leveraged Signature Products To Drive Reach**

Data quickly revealed clear customer favorites.

Content featuring the Crab Rangoon Pizza, specialty pizzas, signature recipes, lunch specials, and unique menu offerings consistently generated some of the highest engagement across platforms.  

Rather than producing generic restaurant content, we doubled down on what customers already loved.

---

## **We Built Consistent Traffic Drivers**

Awareness alone doesn’t fill restaurants.

We developed campaigns around:

* Lunch Specials  
* Game Day Promotions  
* Live Music Events  
* Whiskey Wednesdays  
* Tequila Nights  
* Seasonal Offers  
* Family Meal Deals

These recurring promotions created reasons for customers to return throughout the week rather than only on weekends.

---

## **We Connected Awareness To Revenue**

Social media was only one part of the strategy.

Email marketing, CRM automations, and customer retention campaigns allowed us to continue conversations long after customers left the restaurant.

By consistently nurturing the customer database, Old Shawnee Pizza generated more than 84,000 monthly email opens and an estimated $21,500 in monthly revenue influence through owned marketing channels.

This created a marketing ecosystem focused not only on acquiring customers—but keeping them.

---

# **SCOPE OF WORK**

### **Strategy**

* Brand Positioning  
* Content Strategy  
* Promotional Planning  
* Customer Retention Strategy  
* CRM Development  
* Email Marketing Strategy

### **Content & Execution**

* Social Media Management  
* Photography  
* Video Production  
* Reels & TikTok Content  
* Community Management  
* Graphic Design  
* Copywriting  
* Google Business Optimization  
* Email Campaign Management  
* Marketing Automation

---

# **THE TAKEAWAY**

Old Shawnee Pizza didn’t need a rebrand.

It needed a digital presence that reflected the strength of the brand it had already built over five decades.

By combining storytelling, consistent content production, local market positioning, CRM marketing, and customer retention campaigns, we helped transform a Kansas City institution into a modern digital brand.

**1.66 million impressions.**  
**24,000+ customer actions.**  
**$258,000+ annual revenue influenced.**

Not by changing who Old Shawnee Pizza was.

By making sure more people saw what made it special.

# CHWF

# **CHICK-IN WAFFLE**

## **Building a Restaurant Growth Engine Across Multiple Locations**

### **The Goal**

More customers.  
More repeat visits.  
More revenue.

Through organic content, CRM automation, loyalty marketing, and localized Google Ads, Chick-in Waffle built a system designed to drive awareness, capture demand, and increase customer retention.

---

# **RESULTS AT A GLANCE**

### **10x GOOGLE ADS \- ROAS**

Average return on Google Ads across locations.

### **$9K+**

Average monthly revenue attributed to email marketing.

### **32% Open Rate**

Consistent customer engagement through CRM campaigns and automated flows.

### **5% Conversion Rate**

Turning customer attention into repeat orders.

### **750K+ Impressions**

Generated through organic social media content.

### **20K+ Engagements**

Building awareness and local brand recognition across markets.

### **25K+ Followers**

Across Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok.

### **90% Growth in Engagement**

Driven by founder-led storytelling, community content, and product-focused creative.

---

# **HOW WE DID IT**

### **Made The Food Impossible To Ignore**

We moved beyond traditional food photography and created content designed to stop the scroll. Product launches, food-focused videos, menu features, and visually driven creative turned menu items into attention-grabbing content.

### **Turned The Founder Into A Brand Asset**

Dennis became a recognizable face of the business. Founder-led content consistently generated strong engagement and helped build trust, familiarity, and connection with customers.

### **Showed The People Behind The Brand**

The team, culture, hiring campaigns, community partnerships, and behind-the-scenes moments gave customers something bigger than a menu to connect with.

### **Built A Customer Retention System**

CRM campaigns, loyalty marketing, and automated customer journeys helped keep Chick-in Waffle top-of-mind after the first visit, generating an average of $9K+ in monthly attributed revenue.

### **Captured High-Intent Demand**

Location-specific Google Ads campaigns targeted customers actively searching for dining options nearby, delivering an average 10x return on ad spend.

---

# **SERVICES PROVIDED**

* Social Media Management  
* Content Strategy  
* Founder Storytelling  
* CRM & Email Marketing  
* Marketing Automation  
* Loyalty Marketing  
* Google Ads Management  
* Multi-Location Marketing Strategy

---

**More awareness. More repeat customers. More revenue.**

# AAHA

\`AAHA

# **AAHAA MODERN INDIAN CUISINE**

### **Overland Park, Kansas | Social Media & Brand Positioning**

## **HERO**

**How a Modern Indian Restaurant Generated 460,000+ Organic Impressions by Repositioning Itself as Fine Dining—Not Just Indian Food.**

AAHAA faced a common challenge: consumers often viewed Indian cuisine through a casual dining lens. The opportunity wasn’t to sell dishes—it was to elevate perception.

Our strategy focused on transforming AAHAA into a fine dining experience through visual storytelling, chef-led authority, and premium hospitality content.

---

## **RESULTS AT A GLANCE**

| RESULT | OUTCOME |
| ----- | ----- |
| Organic Impressions | 460,000+ |
| Social Interactions | 3,800+ |
| Instagram Growth | \+19.6% |
| Total Audience | 8,697 Followers |
| Instagram Views | 322,000+ |
| Content Published | 570 Pieces |
| Meta Clicks Generated | 5,128 |
| Ad CTR | 4.18% |

Source: Social performance report, Jan–Jun 2026\.

---

## **HOW WE DID IT**

### **1\. Sold The Experience Before The Food**

Most restaurants showcase menu items. We showcased atmosphere.

Elegant interiors, thoughtful design details, patio moments, and guest experience content positioned AAHAA alongside premium dining destinations rather than traditional ethnic restaurants.

### **2\. Made The Chef Part Of The Brand**

Fine dining is built on credibility.

By highlighting Executive Chef Anjoy Mukherjee, culinary craftsmanship became part of the story. The restaurant gained authority beyond its cuisine and established a stronger premium identity.

### **3\. Elevated Every Dish Into A Visual Statement**

Instead of documenting food, we curated it.

Rich colors, plating details, texture, and presentation transformed dishes into aspirational content that encouraged saves, shares, and repeat engagement.

### **4\. Shifted The Conversation From “Indian Food” To “Modern Dining”**

Every caption, reel, and campaign reinforced one message:

Modern Indian Cuisine. Refined. Contemporary. Elevated.

The content strategy focused on experience, hospitality, and craftsmanship rather than cultural familiarity alone.

### **5\. Built Consistency At Scale**

Premium positioning requires repetition.

Through a high-volume content cadence across Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, Stories, and Reels, the brand stayed visible enough to change perception and build momentum organically.

---

## **SCOPE OF WORK**

| Strategy | Execution |
| ----- | ----- |
| Brand Positioning | Social Media Management |
| Content Strategy | Reels Production |
| Fine Dining Messaging | Photography Direction |
| Audience Growth Strategy | Community Management |
| Campaign Planning | Story Content |
| Performance Analysis | Paid Social Support |

---

### **RESULT**

AAHAA successfully repositioned itself from a restaurant known primarily for its cuisine into a destination recognized for its experience.

The outcome was stronger engagement, accelerated audience growth, increased visibility, and a brand presence that aligns with the expectations of a modern fine dining establishment.

5

# JECA

# **Jerusalem Cafe**

### **Multi-Location Restaurant Group | Kansas City, Missouri**

Jerusalem Cafe has been a Kansas City staple for years.

The challenge wasn’t introducing the restaurant to the market. It was keeping the brand relevant, visible, and top-of-mind in an increasingly competitive dining landscape while creating measurable revenue opportunities through owned marketing channels.

Through content strategy, social media management, and email marketing, we transformed everyday restaurant moments into a consistent growth engine.

---

# **Results at a Glance**

### **565,000+**

Total Impressions Generated

### **25,000+**

Audience Interactions

### **31.7%**

Email Open Rate

**15.5x**

Return on Email Marketing Investment

### **9,900+**

Combined Followers Across Platforms

---

# **How We Did It**

## **We Turned Food Into the Marketing**

The highest-performing content wasn’t promotional.

It was simple, craveable storytelling. Fresh pita coming out of the oven. Signature dishes. Colorful spreads. Texture, movement, and appetite appeal became the foundation of the content strategy.

## **We Showed the People Behind the Brand**

Restaurants are built by people, not menus.

By highlighting staff, kitchen moments, and behind-the-scenes content, we made the brand feel more human while strengthening the connection between the restaurant and its community.

## **We Created Consistent Visibility**

Rather than relying on occasional viral moments, we focused on sustained presence.

A structured content calendar across Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, Google Business Profile, and email ensured the brand stayed visible throughout the customer journey.

## **We Leveraged Owned Media**

Social media created attention.

Email marketing converted that attention into revenue.

With a 31.7% open rate and 117 attributed orders, email became a reliable channel for driving repeat visits and measurable sales.

## **We Focused on Long-Term Brand Equity**

Every piece of content was designed to reinforce what made Jerusalem Cafe successful in the first place:

Authentic food. Genuine hospitality. A local reputation built over years of serving Kansas City.

The strategy wasn’t about changing the brand. It was about making more people experience it.

---

# **Scope of Work**

| Brand & Content | Growth Marketing |
| ----- | ----- |
| Social Media Management | Email Marketing |
| Content Strategy | CRM Campaigns |
| Photography & Video Direction | Audience Retention |
| Community Management | Revenue Attribution |
| Brand Storytelling | Performance Reporting |
| Multi-Platform Distribution | Organic Growth Strategy |

---

# **The Outcome**

By combining consistent content production, strategic storytelling, and retention marketing, Jerusalem Cafe expanded its digital reach, strengthened engagement, and generated measurable revenue without relying on aggressive promotional tactics.

The result was a stronger online presence, increased customer engagement, and a marketing system designed to support long-term growth across multiple locations.

---

**Jerusalem Cafe × Atrium Agency**

*Visibility creates attention. Strategy creates growth.*

# GRCO

### **Building a Lifestyle Brand Through Coffee, Wellness & Community**

#### **Objective**

Position Grand Coffee as more than a coffee shop by highlighting the experiences and values that make the brand unique to young professionals and active consumers.

#### **Brand Pillars**

**Grand Run Club**  
Weekly community-driven runs that foster connection, accountability, and an active lifestyle.

**Functional Smoothies**  
Performance-focused drinks designed to support energy, recovery, and overall wellness.

**Premium Coffee Culture**  
Specialty coffee presented through elevated visuals and everyday moments of productivity.

 **Modern Aesthetics**  
A clean, lifestyle-focused visual identity that reflects the brand’s premium and aspirational positioning.

#### **Creative Approach**

We developed a content strategy centered around real experiences, community engagement, and high-end visual storytelling. Every piece of content reinforced the idea that Grand Coffee is where wellness, productivity, and community come together.

#### **Outcome**

By consistently showcasing the Run Club, functional beverages, premium coffee offerings, and brand aesthetics, we transformed Grand Coffee’s social presence into a lifestyle brand that resonates with health-conscious, ambitious consumers.

# HOTEL KANSAS CITY

### **HOTEL KANSAS CITY**

**Elevating a Historic Property Through Cinematic Storytelling**

#### **Challenge**

Hotel Kansas City needed content that differentiated the property beyond traditional hospitality marketing. Rather than competing on amenities, the goal was to position the hotel as a cultural and experiential destination.

#### **Solution**

The Bottom Studio developed a story-driven content campaign centered on atmosphere, architecture, and the guest experience. Through a cinematic hero film, social-first edits, and visual storytelling assets, we transformed the hotel’s identity into a compelling brand narrative.  

#### **Deliverables**

* 1 Hero Brand Film  
* 1 Atmospheric Film  
* 3 Short-Form Social Cuts  
* 6 Highlight Reels  
* 2-Day Production Shoot  

#### **Outcomes**

* Positioned Hotel Kansas City as a destination rather than simply a place to stay.  
* Created an evergreen content library for social, web, and paid media.  
* Extended campaign reach through multi-platform content distribution.  
* Estimated audience reach of **250K+ viewers**.  

#### **Key Result**

A premium content ecosystem that strengthened brand perception, increased content versatility, and showcased the unique character of Hotel Kansas City.

# TOWN CO

### **THE TOWN COMPANY**

**Building a Culinary Brand Around the People Behind the Experience**

#### **Challenge**

The Town Company needed content that went beyond showcasing dishes and interiors. The goal was to create an emotional connection with the brand by highlighting the personalities, craftsmanship, and culture that define the restaurant experience.

#### **Solution**

We developed a story-driven content campaign centered around **Executive Chef Johnny Leach** and **Helen Jo**, positioning them as the faces of the brand. Through cinematic storytelling, we captured their creative process, leadership, and the energy behind one of Kansas City’s most celebrated dining destinations.

#### **Deliverables**

* Chef-Centered Brand Film  
* Character-Driven Storytelling Assets  
* Social-First Video Cuts  
* Culinary Highlight Reels  
* Behind-the-Scenes Content

#### **Outcomes**

* Humanized the brand through authentic founder and chef storytelling.  
* Elevated The Town Company’s identity beyond food photography and menu promotion.  
* Created a premium content library for social media, PR, and brand marketing.  
* Strengthened the connection between guests and the people behind the dining experience.

#### **Key Result**

By making Johnny Leach and Helen Jo the central characters, the campaign transformed The Town Company from a restaurant people visit into a story people want to be part of.

# Brand Guide — Superside (superside.com)

\# Brand Guide — Superside (superside.com)

A continuación el guide-brand extraído directamente del sitio, con énfasis en el sistema tipográfico (fuente, peso, tamaño y tratamiento).

\#\# 1\. Tipografía

El sistema tipográfico de Superside se construye sobre dos familias complementarias: una sans-serif moderna para todo el contenido funcional y una serif itálica como acento expresivo en titulares.

\*\*Fuente principal (UI \+ Headings):\*\* \`Inter Tight\` (fallbacks: Inter, Arial, sans-serif).  
\*\*Fuente de acento (display):\*\* \`Instrument Serif\` (fallbacks: ui-serif, Georgia, Times New Roman, serif), siempre en estilo \*italic\*, usada dentro de etiquetas \`\<em\>\` para destacar palabras clave en los titulares (por ejemplo, \*"creative team's"\* o \*"AI-first creative"\*).  
\*\*Fuente mono (técnica/secundaria):\*\* \`ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas\`.

Escala tipográfica observada

| Rol | Fuente | Tamaño | Peso | Line-height | Letter-spacing | Estilo |  
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|  
| H1 (hero) | Inter Tight | 60 px | 400 | 72 px | 0.1 px | Regular |  
| H1 — palabra acento (em) | Instrument Serif | 64 px | 400 | 72 px | normal | \*Italic\* |  
| H2 sección | Inter Tight | 48 px | 400 | 56 px | 0.1 px | Regular |  
| H2 — acento (em) | Instrument Serif | 52 px | 400 | 56 px | normal | \*Italic\* |  
| H3 | Inter Tight | 40 px | 400 | 52 px | 0.1 px | Regular |  
| H4 / sub-acentos | Instrument Serif | 32 px | 400 | 36 px | — | \*Italic\* |  
| Body / párrafo | Inter Tight | 16 px | 400 | \~1.5 (24 px) | normal | Regular |  
| Botón / CTA | Inter Tight | 14 px | 600 (semi-bold) | normal | normal | Regular |  
| Nav links | Inter Tight | 16 px | 400 | normal | normal | Regular |  
| Caption / micro | Inter Tight | 12–14 px | 400 | — | — | Regular |

Tamaños adicionales presentes en el sistema: 92, 72, 64, 52, 44, 32, 28, 24, 20, 16, 14, 12 px (escala fluida de display a micro-copy).

Pesos en uso en todo el sitio: solamente \*\*400 (Regular)\*\*, \*\*500 (Medium)\*\* y \*\*600 (Semi-bold)\*\*. No usan Bold (700) ni Black, lo que refuerza una sensación editorial y ligera.

\#\#\# Tratamiento tipográfico (regla de marca clave)

El recurso visual más distintivo es la combinación \*\*sans regular \+ serif italic\*\* dentro del mismo titular. Las palabras emocionales o conceptuales ("\*creative team's\*", "\*AI-first\*", "\*Flexible\*", "\*global creative talent\*") se separan en \`\<em\>\` con Instrument Serif italic, mientras el resto permanece en Inter Tight. Esto crea un contraste editorial (tech \+ craft) que es la firma tipográfica de Superside.

Otras reglas: sin uppercase forzado (\`text-transform: none\` en todos los niveles), tracking casi nulo (0.1 px en headings) y line-heights generosos (\~1.1–1.3 en displays, 1.5 en body).

\#\# 2\. Paleta de color

Construida sobre tokens \`--bor-\*\` (sistema de design tokens):

\- \*\*Primary / Fondo oscuro:\*\* \`\#0A211F\` (rgb 10 33 31\) — verde-negro profundo, fondo principal del hero.  
\- \*\*Primary 600/700:\*\* \`\#081A19\` / \`\#061413\` — variantes más oscuras.  
\- \*\*Secondary / CTA accent:\*\* \`\#D8FF85\` (rgb 216 255 133\) — verde lima, color de acción (botón "Book a demo").  
\- \*\*Secondary 300:\*\* \`\#E4FFAD\` — variante clara del lima.  
\- \*\*Surface clara:\*\* \`\#F7F9F2\` (rgb 247 249 242\) — off-white para texto sobre fondos oscuros y secciones claras.  
\- \*\*Surface alternativa:\*\* \`\#EEF3E4\` / \`\#FAF9F5\` — beiges suaves.  
\- \*\*Tertiary:\*\* \`\#1E3731\` (verde bosque) y \`\#2A4E45\`.  
\- \*\*Texto sobre claro:\*\* \`\#0A211F\`.  
\- \*\*Texto sobre oscuro:\*\* \`\#F7F9F2\`.  
\- \*\*Error:\*\* \`\#961E1E\`.  
\- \*\*Success:\*\* rangos de verdes lima (\`\#C4E878\`, \`\#E4FFAD\`).

\#\# 3\. Botones / Componentes

CTA principal "Book a demo":  
\- Fondo \`\#D8FF85\` (lima), texto \`\#0A211F\`.  
\- Inter Tight, 14 px, peso 600\.  
\- Padding 16 px × 24 px.  
\- Border-radius \*\*999px\*\* (pill completo) — token \`--bor-surface-border-radius: 999px\`.  
\- Sin transformación de texto, sin tracking.

Bordes/radii en general usan el token pill (999px), reforzando un lenguaje suave y orgánico que contrasta con la tipografía geométrica.

\#\# 4\. Logo y marca

Wordmark "Superside" con un símbolo en forma de "S" estilizada a la izquierda, renderizado en blanco (\`\#F7F9F2\`) sobre el fondo oscuro principal. Header con altura fija de 56 px (\`--header-height\`).

\#\# 5\. Personalidad de marca (resumen visual)

La identidad combina seriedad tecnológica (Inter Tight, fondo verde-noche, layouts amplios) con expresividad editorial y artesanal (Instrument Serif italic, lima vibrante como único acento de color, botones pill). El resultado es una marca B2B premium que se siente creativa sin perder claridad funcional.
